label: GMW media-releases + notices

records.notes: High levels first detected July 2020 (month only, used 1st); warning removed October 2021 after 467 days - described as the longest on record for the lake. End date approximated to Alexandra Newspapers publication date 28 Oct 2021. Corroborated by Shepparton News 21 Dec 2020 ('warning persists').
Avoid-contact warning reported by Bendigo Advertiser, first published 25 Jan 2021; exact issue date not stated so start approximated to article date. End/removal date not found.

Red alert (mapped to 'warning') for Bowna Arm at Lake Hume issued 22 Nov 2022 per WaterNSW algae alert page. Corroborated by Border Mail 13 Dec 2022. Included per brief's cross-border exception. No cell count stated.
GMW warning after high levels detected; Bendigo Advertiser first published 11 Jan 2023. Removal date not found.

Warning for Tullaroop Reservoir reported alongside Hepburns Lagoon; Bendigo Advertiser first published 26 Jan 2023. Exact issue date not stated, start approximated to article date. No removal date found.
Warning for Hepburns Lagoon reported alongside Tullaroop Reservoir; Bendigo Advertiser first published 26 Jan 2023. Start approximated to article date. No removal date found.
GMW customer letter dated 21 Feb 2023 for high/potentially toxic blue-green algae at Racecourse Lake and Kangaroo Lake (Torrumbarry Irrigation Area). Removal date not found.
Same GMW customer letter (21 Feb 2023) covering Racecourse Lake with Kangaroo Lake. Removal date not found.
Warning issued December 2023, removed 24 Jan 2024 after 47 days (Shepparton News). Start back-calculated (~8 Dec 2023) from the stated 47-day duration - approximate.
GMW customer letter dated 10 Jan 2024; high levels in Broken River downstream of Lake Nillahcootie (to Casey Weir, incl. Lake Benalla). Removal date not found.
GMW media release states warning issued 27 Feb 2024 and remained in place 127 days. Removal date not directly stated; end date computed as ~3 Jul 2024 from the 127-day duration.
GMW posted the warning on 22 April 2024 (Shepparton News). Warning removed and reported in article published 18 Jul 2024 but exact removal date not stated - end date left null.
Warning issued Monday 9 Dec 2024, removed after 60 days (~7 Feb 2025) per Euroa Gazette. End date computed from 60-day duration; corroborated by GMW 'removed for Lake Eildon feb-25' media release.
Warning (Hepburns Lagoon and Hepburns Race) issued 9 Jan 2025, removed 4 Sep 2025 after 238 days per GMW combined removal notice. Issue date also confirmed by GMW Hepburns notice page.

Warning issued 14 Feb 2025, removed 4 Sep 2025 after 202 days per GMW combined removal notice. Issue date also confirmed by GMW Cairn Curran in-place notice (14 Feb 2025) and Country News 16 Feb 2025.
Warning issued 27 Feb 2025, removed 4 Sep 2025 after 189 days per GMW combined removal notice. (GMW Tullaroop notice page states 28 Feb 2025 - one-day discrepancy in sources.)
GMW media release dated 5 Mar 2025, high levels detected. Removal date not confirmed (a later 'Laanecoorie Reservoir and Tullaroop Creek warning removed' report exists but was not fetched).
Warning issued 20 Mar 2025 (jointly with Lake Boga), removed 30 Apr 2025 after 41 days per GMW notice.

Warning issued 20 Mar 2025 (jointly with Lake Charm) per GMW notice. Lake Boga warning remained much longer than Lake Charm's and was lifted mid-November 2025 (Swan Hill Rural City Council article published 19 Nov 2025); exact removal date not stated so end left null.

GMW warning after high levels detected; Bendigo Advertiser published 11 Apr 2025. A VRFish/GMW notice later confirmed removal 'following a significant decline' but no firm removal date was captured, so end left null.
GMW notice dated 22 Jan 2026, high levels detected. Still listed as an active warning on the GMW alert page (last updated 17 Jul 2026), so end left null.

Warning after significant increases in blue-green algae; Shepparton News published 24 Mar 2026 (start approximated to article date). Mansfield Courier (31 Mar 2026) notes it is the 10th warning at the site since records began in 2004. Still active per GMW alert page (17 Jul 2026).
GMW notice dated Monday 30 Mar 2026, avoid contact after high levels detected. Still listed as active on GMW alert page (17 Jul 2026), so end left null.

Hepburns Lagoon (incl. Hepburns Race) listed as an active blue-green algae warning on the GMW alert page last updated 17 Jul 2026. This is distinct from the 2025 event (removed 4 Sep 2025) but no 2026 issue date was located - start_date null, low confidence.

coverage_notes: Covered GMW-area Victorian waterbodies plus Lake Hume (Bowna Arm) across 2020-2026. Confirmed events by year: 2020 - Lake Eildon (issued Jul 2020, the start of a 467-day bloom). 2021 - Lake Eildon bloom removed Oct 2021; Lake Eppalock (Jan 2021). 2022 - Lake Hume/Bowna Arm red alert (22 Nov 2022); no GMW-specific 2022 warning confirmed (likely a genuine quiet period plus thin online records). 2023 - Lake Eppalock (11 Jan), Tullaroop Reservoir + Hepburns Lagoon (late Jan), Kangaroo Lake + Racecourse Lake (21 Feb). 2024 - Lake Nillahcootie/Broken River (10 Jan), Lake Eildon Dec2023->24 Jan, Cairn Curran (27 Feb->~3 Jul), Lake Eildon (22 Apr->~Jul), Lake Eildon (9 Dec 2024->7 Feb 2025). 2025 - Hepburns Lagoon (9 Jan->4 Sep), Cairn Curran (14 Feb->4 Sep), Tullaroop (27 Feb->4 Sep), Laanecoorie (5 Mar), Lake Charm (20 Mar->30 Apr), Lake Boga (20 Mar->~mid Nov), Lake Eppalock (11 Apr). 2026 - Tullaroop (22 Jan), Lake Eildon (~24 Mar), Lake Eppalock (30 Mar), Hepburns Lagoon (active, date unknown). Waterbodies with NO confirmed bloom in-window despite targeted searches: Waranga Basin (only 2019 events found, outside window), Lake Nagambie, Greens Lake, Lake Buffalo, Newlyn Reservoir, Torrumbarry Weir proper. GAPS/uncertainty: several removal dates are back-calculated from stated durations (noted per record); no explicit cells/mL figure was published in any source (all cell counts null - GMW uses qualitative 'high levels'). Cross-border Lake Hume included per brief; Canadian 'Victoria BC' results were excluded.
caps: GMW reuses stable notice/media-release URL slugs and takes pages down once a warning is lifted: several dated pages (e.g. lake-eildon-april-2024, lake-eildon-dec-24, the generic 'warning-issued-for-lake-eildon' and 'warning-removed-for-lake-eppalock') returned HTTP 404 at fetch time, so some issue/removal dates had to come from regional news or be computed from stated durations. Facebook posts (which carry exact dates) are not fetchable. Inside Water article blocked by robots.txt. Searches for 2022 GMW warnings and for Waranga Basin/Nagambie/Greens Lake in-window returned no confirmable dated bloom, which may reflect thin archival coverage rather than a true absence.

label: MDBA monthly water-quality-threats

records.waterbody: Lake Mulwala (and Murray River, Yarrawonga Weir wall to Tocumwal)
records.notes: High levels of blue-green algae; GMW warned to avoid Lake Mulwala, WaterNSW cautioned the Murray River downstream. Start = article publication date (13 Feb 2020, warning newly issued). No lift date found; end_date null.

records.waterbody: Gippsland Lakes (Lake King, Lake Victoria, Loch Sport, Bunga Arm, Eagle Point, The Entrance)
records.authority: Department of Energy, Environment and Climate Action (Victoria)
records.notes: Toxic Nodularia spumigena bloom. High-level recreational alert plus Do Not Eat Seafood advisory across all Gippsland Lakes. Detected/warned ~14 April 2022 (Easter), high through 17 May 2022 update; declined to low level by 10 June 2022 update (seafood advisory continued). end_date left null as recreational warning wound down gradually June 2022.

records.authority: Department of Energy, Environment and Climate Action (Victoria)
records.notes: Part of the 2022 Gippsland Lakes Nodularia bloom. Warning for Lake Wellington was reported lifted by 14 April 2022 as counts fell; exact issue date not stated so start set to 1 March 2022 (month approximate). Low confidence.

Red alert (avoid contact) for untreated water at Hume Dam/Lake Hume. Border waterbody straddling VIC/NSW, relevant to Victorians. Start = article publication date 13 Dec 2022 (advisory newly issued). No lift date confirmed.
High levels detected, avoid direct water contact. Start = article publication date 11 Jan 2023 (warning newly issued). No lift date found.
High levels at Tullaroop Reservoir and Hepburns Lagoon reported together. Start = article publication date 26 Jan 2023 (warnings newly issued in January 2023). No lift date found.
High levels at Hepburns Lagoon and Tullaroop Reservoir reported together, January 2023. Start = article publication date 26 Jan 2023. No lift date found.

High levels, avoid contact. Article (28 Jan 2023) notes two additional central-Vic storages detected the same algae earlier that week (part of the Jan 2023 Eppalock/Tullaroop/Hepburns outbreak). Start = publication date; no lift date found.
Warning issued December 2023, in place 47 days, lifted 24 Jan 2024. Start (2023-12-08) back-calculated from 47 days before 24 Jan 2024, approximate to within a few days.
GMW media release: warning issued 27 Feb 2024, remained in place 127 days. End date (~3 July 2024) computed from 127-day duration; approximate to within a day.
Warning posted 22 April 2024, lifted 18 July 2024 (~88 days). Explicit issue and removal dates from GMW-sourced news report.
Toxic bloom confirmed by City of Greater Bendigo environmental health coordinator; avoid contact, keep dogs leashed. Start = article date 9 Oct 2024. No lift date found.
GMW: warning issued 9 Dec 2024, in place 60 days, removed 11 Feb 2025. Explicit dates.

Secondary mention in a 12 May 2025 article: Hepburns Lagoon received an alert on 9 Jan 2025 (first of the 2025 central-Vic season). Not the primary subject of the article; low confidence. No lift date found.
GMW notice dated 20 March 2025: high levels at Lake Boga and Lake Charm, avoid contact. No lift date found.
GMW notice dated 20 March 2025: high levels at Lake Charm and Lake Boga, avoid contact. No lift date found.
High levels detected, avoid contact; warning issued 11 April 2025. Still high (with Cairn Curran and Laanecoorie) as of 9 May 2025 per follow-up article. No lift date found.
Closed to on-water recreation since 23 April 2025 due to blue-green algae (per 12 May 2025 article). No lift date found.

High levels confirmed as of 9 May 2025 (article 12 May 2025), alongside Lake Eppalock and Laanecoorie. Exact issue date not stated; start = confirmed high-level date 9 May 2025. Separate event from the Feb-Jul 2024 Cairn Curran warning. No lift date found.
High levels confirmed as of 9 May 2025 (article 12 May 2025), alongside Lake Eppalock and Cairn Curran. Exact issue date not stated; start = confirmed high-level date. No lift date found.
GMW warnings list (fetched 17 July 2026): warning issued 13 Nov 2025, still active as of 17 July 2026. Distinct from the Jan 2025 Hepburns Lagoon event.
GMW warnings list (fetched 17 July 2026): warning issued 22 Jan 2026, still active as of 17 July 2026.

GMW warnings list (fetched 17 July 2026): warning issued 23 March 2026, still active as of 17 July 2026. Corroborated by Shepparton News (24 March 2026); described as the 10th Eildon alert since 2004. Applies to all locations incl. Main Arm, Goughs Bay, Jamieson, Delatite, Bonnie Doon.
GMW warnings list (fetched 17 July 2026): warning issued 30 March 2026, still active as of 17 July 2026. Corroborated by Bendigo Advertiser (31 March 2026) as the 14th Eppalock warning in 22 years.

coverage_notes:
Covered 2020, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025 and 2026 for Victorian and VIC/NSW-border waterbodies. 25 distinct bloom events confirmed by fetching a dated authoritative or news page for each. Waterbodies covered: Lake Eppalock (Jan 2023, Apr 2025, Mar 2026 - three separate events), Lake Eildon (Dec2023-Jan2024, Apr-Jul2024, Dec2024-Feb2025, Mar2026 - four events), Cairn Curran (2024, 2025), Tullaroop (2023, 2026), Hepburns Lagoon (Jan2023, Jan2025, Nov2025), Laanecoorie (2023, 2025), Upper Coliban (2025), Lake Boga & Lake Charm (2025), Lake Neangar/Eaglehawk (2024), Lake Mulwala (2020), Lake Hume (border, 2022), Gippsland Lakes + Lake Wellington (2022). GAPS: (1) 2021 - no confirmable Victorian bloom event was surfaced by multiple searches; 2021 was a wet La Nina summer that likely suppressed blooms, but this is an inferred gap not a confirmed absence. (2) Full 2020 and early-2021 GMW notice history is not archived (GMW notice URLs are live-overwritten and 404 once superseded), so pre-2022 GMW coverage relies on news reports and is likely incomplete. (3) Gippsland Lakes only firmly covered for the 2022 bloom; other years not confirmed. (4) Lower Murray Water region (Mildura/Merbein Murray River blooms) and GWMWater (Wimmera-Mallee) lakes were identified as sources but no specific dated events within 2020-2026 were confirmed. Canada/Victoria BC false positives were excluded throughout. No cell counts (cells/mL) were stated on any fetched source, so all cell_count fields are null.</coverage_notes>
<parameter name="caps">MDBA source stream was UNAVAILABLE: every mdba.gov.au monthly 'Water quality threats' page and the standing blue-green algae page returned ROBOTS_DISALLOWED via WebFetch on repeated attempts, and the Wayback Machine mirror returned HTTP 403 (PROXY_REJECTED). The requested MDBA month-by-month reconstruction could therefore not be performed; I substituted Goulburn-Murray Water authority pages plus dated regional news (Bendigo Advertiser, Border Mail, Country News, Shepparton News) and water.vic.gov.au. GMW notice/media-release URLs frequently 404 once a warning is superseded (live-overwritten CMS), so several events were confirmed via dated news mirrors instead of the original authority page. WaterNSW algae-alert archive URLs (2022/2023) also 404 - reorganised. Searches for 2021 ran dry after multiple query variations.</caps>
</invoke>

label: WaterNSW Lake Hume alerts

records.notes: Red alert blue-green algae warning issued for the whole of Lake Hume; Border Mail (pub 25 Feb 2020) states the alert was issued 'on Monday' = 24 Feb 2020. No lift date located; end_date null. No cell count stated. WaterNSW is the issuing authority; corroborated by dated news. Lake Hume straddles VIC/NSW border and is included per instructions.

records.region: Yarrawonga Weir, Murray River, VIC-NSW border
records.notes: High levels of blue-green algae detected at Yarrawonga Weir / Lake Mulwala; warning issued mid-Feb 2020. start_date = report date (Federation Council notice pub 14 Feb 2020; Border Mail story 6629845 pub 13 Feb 2020) — actual detection likely a day or two earlier. An additional amber 'caution' was added for the Murray River downstream of Yarrawonga weir wall to Tocumwal (not a separate Victorian waterbody record). Lake Mulwala is on the Murray VIC/NSW border. No cell count stated; no lift date located. Corroborating Border Mail URL: https://www.bordermail.com.au/story/6629845/blue-green-algae-warning-for-lake-mulwala/

records.region: Hume Weir (whole storage), Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
records.notes: WaterNSW issued a red alert ('high alert') for the whole of Hume Weir on Christmas Eve, 24 Dec 2021 (Border Mail pub 25 Dec 2021). No lift date located (would have lifted early 2022); end_date null. No cell count stated.

records.region: Hume Dam, Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
records.notes: Red alert issued Tuesday 10 Oct 2022 (Border Mail 7939895, surface scums widespread across the storage) and downgraded/lifted 14 Nov 2022 per the WaterNSW 'red alert warning lifted – Hume Dam' page (fetched, pub 14 Nov 2022) and Border Mail 7980762 (pub 14 Nov 2022, 'red alert has been downgraded'). No cell count stated. WaterNSW authority page confirms the lift; issue date from dated news.

records.region: Bowna Arm, Lake Hume, VIC-NSW border
records.notes: Red alert (high levels) issued for Bowna Arm of Lake Hume; WaterNSW page fetched, publication date 22 Nov 2022. Same page noted Amber alerts concurrently at Dam wall, Ebden, Bethanga and below the resort. No cell count stated. No specific lift date located; end_date null. Bowna Arm straddles the VIC/NSW border and is included per instructions.

records.region: Hume Dam, Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
records.notes: 'Another' red alert issued for Lake Hume; Border Mail 8017868 (pub 13 Dec 2022) states issued 'on Tuesday' = 13 Dec 2022. Border Mail 8046291 (pub 12 Jan 2023) confirms the red alert remained in place from mid-December into January 2023. A WaterNSW 2023 'Red Alert blue-green algal warning lifted for Hume Dam' page exists (URL: https://www.waternsw.com.au/water-services/water-quality/algae-alerts/algae-alerts/2023/red-alert-blue-green-algal-warning-lifted-for-hume-dam) and would mark the lift of this event, but it now returns HTTP 404 so the exact lift date is unconfirmed; end_date null. No cell count stated.

records.region: Hume Dam at Frist Bay Creek crossing of the Murray Valley Highway near Tallangatta, VIC-NSW border
records.notes: WaterNSW red alert for Hume Dam at Frist Bay Creek crossing (Murray Valley Highway near Tallangatta). Issued page filed under /2023/ and a corresponding 'lifted' page filed under /2024/ (URL: https://www.waternsw.com.au/water-services/water-quality/algae-alerts/algae-alerts/2024/blue-green-algae-red-alert-lifted-for-hume-dam-at-frist-bay-creek-crossing-of-the-murray-valley-highway-near-tallangatta), indicating a bloom spanning the 2023-24 summer. Both pages now return HTTP 404 on fetch — event and year are taken from the WaterNSW search-index titles only, so no exact day could be confirmed; start_date and end_date null, confidence low. No cell count stated.

records.region: Hume Dam at Ebden, Upper Murray / VIC-NSW border
records.notes: Red alert issued Friday 22 Aug 2025 for Lake Hume at Ebden; lifted (returned to green/low) 8 Sep 2025 — 'red alert lifted more than two weeks after blue-green algae found'. Source: Border Mail 9047577 (pub 8 Sep 2025). A WaterNSW page exists (https://www.waternsw.com.au/water-services/water-quality/algae-alerts/algae-alerts/2025/blue-green-algae-red-alert-for-hume-dam-at-ebden) but returns HTTP 404 on fetch. No cell count stated.

coverage_notes: Covered WaterNSW border storages relevant to Victoria for 2020-2026: Lake Hume (incl. Hume Dam, Bowna Arm, Ebden, Frist Bay Creek/Tallangatta) and Lake Mulwala/Yarrawonga Weir. Confirmed events by year: 2020 — Lake Hume red alert (24 Feb) and Lake Mulwala/Yarrawonga Weir red alert (mid-Feb); 2021 — Lake Hume whole-weir red alert (24 Dec, Christmas Eve); 2022 — Lake Hume red alert (10 Oct, lifted 14 Nov), Bowna Arm red alert (22 Nov), Lake Hume red alert (13 Dec, ran into Jan 2023); 2023-24 — Hume Dam at Frist Bay Creek red alert (season-spanning, exact dates unconfirmed); 2025 — Lake Hume at Ebden red alert (22 Aug – 8 Sep). GAPS: No confirmed border-storage events found for 2021 (Jan-Nov) or for the 2024-25 summer (Dec 2024–early 2025) — either none occurred or reporting wasn't indexed. Lift dates are missing for the 2020 Lake Hume, 2020 Lake Mulwala, 2021 Lake Hume, 2022 Bowna Arm, and 2022-Dec Lake Hume events (end_date null). No source stated any explicit cell count in cells/mL, so all cell counts are null. All Canadian 'Victoria BC' results were excluded. WaterNSW alerts for other far-western Murray storages (Wentworth/Sunraysia RACC reports) were seen in search but not fetched as they are downstream and less directly Victorian.
caps: WaterNSW only retains a rolling window of algae-alert pages: most archived alert URLs (2022 Lake Hume issued, 2023 issued+lifted Frist Bay, 2024 lifted, 2025 Ebden) now return HTTP 404 even though they appear in the search index with fully descriptive titles — so exact issued/lifted days for several events could not be pulled from the authority page and were taken from Border Mail instead. WaterNSW yearly index pages (…/algae-alerts/algae-alerts/2022 etc.) also 404. The Internet Archive (web.archive.org) was blocked by the agent proxy (HTTP 403), so Wayback fallback was unavailable. Facebook WaterNSW posts (which carry exact issued/lifted dates) were blocked by robots.txt. Border Mail article bodies are partly paywalled; publication dates and lead paragraphs were readable and used for dating.

label: GWMWater / LMW / SRW / MelbWater

records.notes: GWMWater warning to avoid direct contact after testing detected blue-green algae above safe recreation levels. start_date is the article publication date (16 Nov 2020) announcing the warning; exact issue date not separately stated. A GWMWater media release later declared the outbreak 'over' (lift date not confirmed), so end_date left null.
Warning issued 20 Jan 2023 advising avoid direct contact; boating/sightseeing still permitted. No numeric cell count published. Lift date not stated.
GWMWater warning that algae exceeded safe recreation levels; avoid direct water contact. start_date = article date 17 May 2023. No numeric cell count. Lift date not stated.

Warning confirmed ACTIVE as of the article date 29 Apr 2025 (blue-green algae above safe recreation levels); actual issue date earlier and unknown, so 2025-04-29 is a latest-bound estimate, not the true onset. Distinct 2025 event separate from the 2020 Lonsdale warning.
Warning confirmed ACTIVE as of 29 Apr 2025 (avoid direct contact). start_date is the report date, not confirmed onset. No numeric cell count. Which 'Green Lake' - GWMWater's Green Lake near Horsham.
Warning confirmed ACTIVE as of 29 Apr 2025. Distinct from the Jan 2023 Lake Wallace warning. start_date is report date, true onset unknown.

Same 29 Apr 2025 article reports Taylors Lake warning had been LIFTED, implying an earlier 2025 warning that ended by 29 Apr 2025. No issue date and no exact lift date given; end_date set to the report date as an approximate upper bound. Low confidence - reconstructed from a 'lifted' mention only.

records.waterbody: Murray River (Psyche Bend to Merbein) and Kings Billabong
records.notes: RED ALERT (=warning) over a ~20km stretch Psyche Bend to Merbein plus Kings Billabong. Testing by Lower Murray Water; WaterNSW co-monitors the border river. Kings Billabong and Psyche Bend are Victorian waterbodies. start_date = published 6 Jul 2025; alert expected to persist at least 14 days. No numeric cell count published.

RED ALERT (=warning) issued 27 Feb 2026 by Lower Murray Water for high levels of potentially toxic blue-green algae; attributed to hot humid weather with levels fluctuating weekly. Corroborated by Sunraysia Daily (27 Feb 2026). No numeric cell count. Lift date not yet known as of research date.

Nodularia spumigena bloom across Gippsland Lakes (Lake Victoria, Lake King, Loch Sport, Bunga Arm, etc.), spreading along Ninety Mile Beach. 'Do Not Eat Seafood' advisory plus gut-and-gill fish advisory; high toxin levels detected in seafood. water.vic.gov.au update last updated 17 May 2022; DHHS health warning re-issued 27 May 2022. This is a DHHS/health-stream event (not a water-corporation storage), included here because confirmed; likely overlaps another source stream for dedup. No numeric cell count.

coverage_notes:
STREAM = 'other Victorian water corporations' (GWMWater, Lower Murray Water, Southern Rural Water, Melbourne Water), 1 Jan 2020 - 18 Jul 2026.

CONFIRMED EVENTS by corporation:
- GWMWater (Wimmera-Mallee): Lake Lonsdale (Nov 2020; and again active Apr 2025), Lake Wallace/Edenhope (Jan 2023; and again active Apr 2025), Taylors Lake (May 2023; a further 2025 warning lifted by 29 Apr 2025), Green Lake (active Apr 2025). All confirmed via GWMWater/West Wimmera Shire Council pages and The Weekly Advertiser (Horsham). Note the 29 Apr 2025 article bundles Lonsdale+Green Lake+Lake Wallace as simultaneously active - a multi-lake autumn 2025 Wimmera bloom.
- Lower Murray Water (Sunraysia/Mallee): Lake Cullulleraine RED ALERT 27 Feb 2026 (LMW authority page + Sunraysia Daily); Murray River Psyche Bend-to-Merbein + Kings Billabong RED ALERT 6 Jul 2025 (Sunraysia Daily). LMW's own BGA page is an interactive map with no text history (sampling suspended 28 May 2026, resumes Oct 2026).
- Southern Rural Water (Gippsland/southern: Blue Rock, Glenmaggie, Rosslynne, Pykes Creek, Merrimu, Melton, Narracan): SRW warnings page shows 'no current warnings' and NO dated historical recreational BGA warnings were found for 2020-2026 via news (Gippsland Times / web). GAP - either genuinely few reportable events or not indexed.
- Melbourne Water (metro reservoirs/waterways): NO dated blue-green algae recreational warning events found for 2020-2026. GAP.

Plus one cross-stream event captured: Gippsland Lakes Nodularia bloom May 2022 (DHHS/health.vic seafood advisory) - likely belongs to a health-department stream; included for completeness, flag for dedup.

YEAR COVERAGE: 2020 (1 event), 2022 (1, cross-stream), 2023 (2), 2025 (4-5), 2026 (1). GAPS: 2021 and 2024 - no confirmable recreational BGA warnings surfaced for the four target corporations in those years (2024 Murray results in the Balranald RACC Sept-2024 report were only GREEN/low-level surveillance at Victorian sites Merbein/Swan Hill, not warnings). A Western Murray Irrigation AMBER drinking-water supply alert for Merbein (Victorian town) dated 13 Dec 2024 exists but concerns the main-stem Murray drinking supply, not a Victorian recreational waterbody, so it was NOT recorded.

CELL COUNTS: none of the Victorian authority/news pages published explicit cells/mL figures for these events, so all cell_count fields are null (never invented).

DATE PRECISION: 2025 Wimmera lakes have start_date = report date (29 Apr 2025) as a latest-bound, since GWMWater/news announced them 'active' without an onset date - flagged in each note.
caps: Authority warning pages (GWMWater, SRW, LMW) only display CURRENT warnings, not history - LMW is an interactive map with no readable text history. Wayback CDX API was blocked by the agent proxy (HTTP 403) and MDBA monthly water-quality-threats pages were robots.txt-blocked, closing off two archive routes. Historical reconstruction therefore relied on regional news (The Weekly Advertiser, Sunraysia Daily) and council pages, which under-cover 2021 and 2024 and likely miss shorter/localised warnings, especially Southern Rural Water (Gippsland) and Melbourne Water (metro) where searches ran dry. Sunraysia Daily and Inside Water articles were partly paywalled/robots-blocked.

label: DEECA / water.vic / Gippsland Lakes / EPA

records.notes: High levels of blue-green algae; public warned to avoid direct contact. Article first published 25 Jan 2021 (Bendigo Advertiser). No cell count stated. End date unknown.

records.waterbody: Gippsland Lakes (Lake King and Lake Victoria)
records.notes: Toxic Nodularia spumigena bloom. Avoid-contact warning for Lake King and Lake Victoria incl. Paynesville, Eagle Point, Newlands Arm, Wattle Point, Metung. Lake Wellington recreational warning lifted by this date but seafood advisory remained. Confirmed continuing via water.vic 17 May 2022 update (Do Not Eat Seafood advisory across all Gippsland Lakes) at https://www.water.vic.gov.au/waterways-and-catchments/rivers-estuaries-and-waterways/blue-green-algae/2022-reports/may-2022-gippsland-lakes-algae-update ; by the June 2022 update Nodularia had declined to 'low'. No cell count stated in sources.

Red alert (=warning) for high levels of blue-green algae at Bowna Arm of Lake Hume, issued 22 Nov 2022 by WaterNSW. Lake Hume straddles VIC/NSW border. No cell count in the page. End/lift date not confirmed (a 'red alert lifted for Hume Dam' 2023 page exists but returned 404 on fetch).

Second red-alert advisory for Lake Hume since October 2022 (first lasted over a month); reported by Border Mail, first published 13 Dec 2022. May be a continuation of the same 2022-23 Lake Hume bloom series as the Bowna Arm alert; counted separately as a distinct red-alert period. No cell count.
High levels of blue-green algae detected; avoid-contact warning. Bendigo Advertiser first published 11 Jan 2023. No cell count. End date unknown.
GMW warning issued 9 Dec 2024, remained in place 60 days, lifted 11 Feb 2025 (dates attributed to Goulburn-Murray Water). No cell count.
First 2025 warning in the central Vic wave issued 9 Jan 2025 (per Bendigo Advertiser 12 May 2025); GMW removed the warning 4 Sep 2025 along with Cairn Curran and Tullaroop. No cell count.

GMW customer letter dated 14 Feb 2025: high blue-green algae, potentially toxic. Warning removed 4 Sep 2025 per GMW notice (https://www.g-mwater.com.au/news-updates/notices/blue-green-algae-warnings-removed-for-cairn-curran-reservoir-tullaroop-reservoir-and-hep.html). No cell count.

Tullaroop included in the GMW warning removal of 4 Sep 2025 (with Cairn Curran and Hepburns Lagoon). Exact 2025 issue date not confirmed (part of the Feb-May 2025 central Vic wave). End date confirmed 4 Sep 2025. No cell count.

Council media release published 4 Apr 2025: avoid swimming/direct contact with algae-affected water at Lake Victoria, Maryborough. This is the Maryborough Lake Victoria (a small urban lake), NOT the Gippsland Lake Victoria. No cell count. End unknown.

High levels detected; avoid contact. Bendigo Advertiser 11 Apr 2025. Still active as of 9 May 2025 per Bendigo Advertiser 12 May 2025 (https://www.bendigoadvertiser.com.au/story/8964408/). Treated as one continuous Apr-May 2025 event. No cell count.
Closed to on-water recreation since 23 Apr 2025 due to blue-green algae, per Bendigo Advertiser 12 May 2025. No cell count. End unknown.

Named among central Victorian waterbodies with high blue-green algae 'as of May 9' 2025 (Bendigo Advertiser 12 May 2025). Start date approximate (1st detection date not explicitly given; used the 9 May reference date). No cell count. End unknown.

A DEECA 'Gippsland Lakes Algae Update - 31 May 2024' (and a '15 March 2024' update) appear in water.vic.gov.au search results, indicating a 2024 Gippsland Lakes algae season, but BOTH PDFs returned HTTP 404 when fetched, so lakes/levels/cell counts could not be confirmed. Date is the report title date, not a verified bloom start. Low confidence - flagged for re-verification.
New warning issued 13 Nov 2025 (distinct from the Jan-Sep 2025 warning). Still active as of 17 Jul 2026 per GMW warnings page. No cell count.
Warning issued 22 Jan 2026; still active as of 17 Jul 2026 per GMW warnings page. Distinct from the 2025 Tullaroop warning. No cell count.
Warning issued 23 Mar 2026; still active as of 17 Jul 2026 per GMW warnings page. No cell count.

GMW notice: warning issued Monday 30 Mar 2026, high levels of blue-green algae, avoid contact. Still active as of 17 Jul 2026 per GMW warnings page (https://www.g-mwater.com.au/water-operations/bga). No cell count.

Council media release published 13 May 2026: do not swim / avoid direct contact with blue-green algae affected water at Lake Victoria, Maryborough. Distinct from the 4 Apr 2025 warning. No cell count. End unknown.

coverage_notes: Covered 1 Jan 2020 - 18 Jul 2026, Victoria. Confirmed 20 distinct bloom/warning periods by fetching dated authority or news pages. YEARS: 2020 - NO confirmed events found (searches for Gippsland Lakes 2020 / Lake Wellington 2020 surfaced only undated pages and a DELWP Facebook post that could not be fetched; this is a genuine gap, not a confirmed absence). 2021 - Lake Eppalock (Jan). 2022 - strong: Gippsland Lakes (Lake King/Lake Victoria, Nodularia spumigena, Apr-Jun, confirmed via news + water.vic May 2022 update), Lake Hume/Bowna Arm red alert (Nov), Lake Hume 2nd red alert (Dec). 2023 - Lake Eppalock (Jan). 2024 - Lake Eildon (issued Dec, ran into 2025); a DEECA Gippsland Lakes 2024 season is indicated by water.vic report titles (15 Mar & 31 May 2024) but the PDFs 404'd so it is only a LOW-confidence flagged record. 2025 - heavy central-Victoria season: Hepburns Lagoon (Jan-Sep), Cairn Curran (Feb-Sep), Tullaroop (-Sep), Lake Victoria Maryborough (Apr), Lake Eppalock (Apr-May), Upper Coliban (Apr-), Laanecoorie (May), plus new Hepburns warning from Nov. 2026 - Tullaroop (Jan), Lake Eildon (Mar), Lake Eppalock (Mar), Lake Victoria Maryborough (May); four GMW warnings still active on 17 Jul 2026. LAKES well covered: Lake Eppalock, Lake Eildon, Cairn Curran, Tullaroop, Hepburns Lagoon, Laanecoorie, Upper Coliban (all Goulburn-Murray Water storages), Lake Hume/Bowna Arm (VIC/NSW border), Lake Victoria Maryborough (council), Gippsland Lakes system. GAPS: (1) 2020 and most of 2021 - little in search results; likely under-reported here. (2) Gippsland Lakes seasonal reports for 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026 - the water.vic report PDFs mostly 404'd, so Gippsland is well-documented only for 2022; explicit cyanobacteria cell counts (cells/mL) from the DEECA seasonal reports could NOT be retrieved for ANY year because those PDFs were unreachable. (3) NO explicit cell-count numbers were confirmable from any fetched source, so every cell_count field is null (none invented). (4) Metro Melbourne / western district lakes (Wendouree, Colac, Albert Park) not confirmed - a targeted search returned only US results. Canada (Victoria BC) results (victoriabuzz.com etc.) were seen and deliberately EXCLUDED.
caps: Multiple water.vic.gov.au Gippsland Lakes seasonal-report PDFs (e.g. Gippsland-Lakes-algae-update-31May-2024.pdf, 15-March-2024.pdf) returned HTTP 404 on WebFetch despite appearing in search results - blocked retrieval of Gippsland cell-count data and 2023-2026 Gippsland confirmations. The WaterNSW 'red alert lifted for Hume Dam 2023' page also 404'd, so Lake Hume end dates are unconfirmed. GMW notice pages appear to show only the LATEST notice per URL (the generic 'warning-for-lake-eppalock' URL now resolves to the 30 Mar 2026 event), so historical GMW notices had to be recovered via news outlets instead. WebSearch is US-biased: a Lake Wendouree/Colac query returned entirely US HAB pages, and 'Victoria' queries repeatedly surfaced Victoria BC Canada results (filtered out). A DELWP Gippsland Facebook post about a 2020 Lake Wellington bloom was found but Facebook could not be fetched. Search did not run dry on major GMW storages but did run thin for 2020-2021 across all sources.

label: Metro councils

records.notes: Blue-green algae bloom warning at the Wilson Botanic Park lake; council advised avoiding contact and keeping pets away. Warning reported issued Monday 4 May 2026 (Berwick/Cranbourne Star News, published 8-9 May 2026). Council began treatment with beneficial bacteria expected to take ~3 weeks, so likely active until ~late May 2026. No end/lift date confirmed; no cell counts stated. Bloom attributed to warm weather, sunlight, low water movement and elevated nutrients.

Hume City Council media release. Blue-green algae bloom confirmed at Spavin Lake, Sunbury on 7 January 2025; warning to avoid water contact. Follow-up inspection 7 February 2025 found no visible signs of algae, but warning not formally lifted ('conditions can change rapidly'). end_date 2025-02-07 is the 'no visible signs' date, used as best estimate of clearance. Related council Instagram/Facebook 'algae update' posts appear from ~Dec 2024, suggesting the bloom may have begun late Dec 2024. No cell counts stated.

Sunbury Life report. Council last tested water in March 2023 and blue-green algae was detected, with warning signs erected. Only the month (March) is known for detection, so start_date set to 2023-03-01 (1st of month, approximate). Re-test around 15 May 2023 showed algae no longer present (end_date). No severity classification or cell counts given.

Star Mail (mailcommunity) report, 20 Feb 2026. Melbourne Water testing found toxic blue-green algae levels 'low' at Lillydale Lake at the time, not currently a severe health risk, but warned conditions can change rapidly; Yarra Ranges Council continues to advise against swimming. Classified 'watch' because levels were reported low (surveillance-level), not a full avoid-contact alert. start_date is the report date; actual detection date not stated. No cell counts given.

EPA Victoria Facebook post titled 'Avoid contact with water in Edwardes Lake Park, Reservoir' (blue-green algae follow-up). Confirms a real avoid-contact warning event at Edwardes Lake, but the post could NOT be fetched (Facebook blocked by robots.txt), so the exact date is unconfirmed - only the search-result title was seen. Facebook post ID (~994977xxx) is close in magnitude to a Hume post from 2026, suggesting a likely 2026 event, but this is not confirmable. start_date left null; confidence low. Darebin's own current-status page (darebin.vic.gov.au Algal-blooms-at-Edwardes-Lake) showed 'no toxic blue-green algae' at time of check with no historical dates.

coverage_notes: Assigned source stream = Greater-Melbourne council-issued blue-green algae warnings, 2020-2026. Covered all 9 target council/lake pairs. Confirmed dated events: Wilson Botanic Park Lake / Casey (May 2026, news); Spavin Lake / Hume (Jan-Feb 2025 via Hume media release, high confidence; and March-May 2023 via Sunbury Life); Lillydale Lake / Yarra Ranges (Feb 2026, low-level 'watch'); Edwardes Lake / Darebin (EPA warning, date unconfirmable - Facebook robots-blocked). KEY GAP: most council pages are live 'current status' resources with NO historical archive of dated warnings - Whitehorse (Blackburn Lake, Surrey Dive), Nillumbik (Yarrambat Lake - noted as a frequent-bloom site with permanent signage but no dated events published), Knox (Quarry Lake - only enterococci/bacteria noted, page dated 11 May 2026), and Glen Eira (Caulfield Park Lake - only a passing mention) published no dated bloom events I could confirm. These urban lakes bloom often but warnings are poorly/inconsistently dated online, so the true event count is certainly higher than the 5 recorded here. Years with confirmed events: 2023, 2025, 2026. No confirmable council-lake events found for 2020, 2021, 2022, 2024 (2024 likely covered by the Dec-2024/Jan-2025 Spavin bloom recorded under 2025). Deliberately EXCLUDED false positives: UK 'Surrey' blue-green algae articles (Surrey Dive search returned getsurrey.co.uk), and numerous US HAB pages (Kansas, Oregon, California, Indiana). No Canada/Victoria-BC results were included.
caps: Facebook and Instagram sources repeatedly blocked by robots.txt (WebFetch ROBOTS_DISALLOWED) - could not confirm dates for the EPA Edwardes Lake post nor for Hume/Spavin social 'algae update' posts (one Hume Facebook post id 996655579162913 reads 'we're responding to a blue-green algae [bloom]' and may indicate a further Spavin event in ~2025-2026 that I could not date). Council authority pages are current-status only with no dated historical archives, so pre-2023 events for these urban lakes are effectively unretrievable via web search - the search ran dry for 2020-2022 council-lake warnings despite multiple year-specific queries. Recommend the merge step cross-check EPA Victoria's official BGA notification archive and council social feeds for the many undated urban-lake blooms not captured here.

label: Regional councils

records.notes: Council media release dated 27 March 2024: samples confirmed 'high levels of the toxic algae' at Green Lake near Horsham. No lift date stated on this page. No cells/mL figure given.

Regional masthead (The Weekly Advertiser, 29 April 2025) lists Green Lake among Wimmera lakes with an active BGA warning ('above levels considered safe for recreation use') alongside Lake Lonsdale and Lake Wallace. Exact issue date not stated; start_date set to 1 April 2025 as a month-only placeholder (bloom active as of 29 Apr 2025). Distinct from the March 2024 Green Lake event.

Council media release dated 28 February 2024: lab analysis found 'an unacceptable level of blue green algae species'; advised avoiding all direct contact until further advice. No lift date or cells/mL figure on this page.
Council notice dated Friday 7 November 2025: 'high levels of blue-green algae' detected at Kialla Lakes. No lift date or cells/mL figure given.

Council notice dated Tuesday 16 January 2024: monitoring 'detected high levels of blue-green algae' at Lake Bartlett, Tatura. A later council 'levels decline' page marks the end but its date was not captured (WebFetch rate-limited). No cells/mL figure.
Council media release dated 4 April 2025: a BGA bloom affecting Lake Victoria, Maryborough; advised avoiding contact. No lift date or cells/mL figure.
Council media release dated 13 May 2026: BGA bloom affecting Lake Victoria attributed to lack of rain and warm late-autumn weather. Distinct event from the April 2025 warning. No cells/mL figure.

Swan Hill Rural City Council release (published 19 November 2025) welcomes GMW lifting the Lake Boga BGA warning ahead of summer; Gannawarra Times reported the lift 20 Nov 2025. Original issue date not stated (2025). end_date approximated to 19 Nov 2025.

Bendigo Advertiser article published 8 October 2024 (updated 9 Oct): BGA bloom at Lake Neangar verified by City of Greater Bendigo environmental health coordinator; residents advised to avoid contact. No cells/mL figure. Council did not have a separate dated release located.

Council page published 18 March 2024: 'Regular testing has detected levels of blue-green algae above levels considered safe for recreation use' at Lake Wallace. No lift date on this page. Distinct from the 2025 event.

Warning lifted per West Wimmera Shire Council page dated 12 June 2025 ('Recent testing indicates the water is safe'). Issue date ~19 March 2025 taken from Wimmera Mallee News article slug/date (wimmeramalleenews.com.au/blue-green-algae-in-lake-wallace-2025-03-19); issue-date confidence medium, lift-date confidence high. Council 2025 warning page existed but could not be fetched (rate-limited).

Ararat Rural City Council has a media release titled 'Blue-green algae warning issued for Lake Bolac' confirming a BGA warning event, but the page could not be fetched to confirm the exact date (ararat.vic.gov.au repeatedly returned proxy 429). Event is real; date unconfirmed. Related coverage: araratadvertiser.com.au stories 7081813 and 7086684 (approx 2021).

City of Ballarat news page 'Blue-green algae closes Lake Burrumbeet': water samples showed Dolichospermum at a biovolume of 8.653 mm3/L (biovolume, NOT cells/mL, so cell counts left null); lake closed until safe. The page carried no visible publication date; corroborating dated reports (3BA, Power FM Ballarat, Ballarat City Council Facebook) could not be fetched (rate-limited). Likely a recent (2024-2025) closure but date unconfirmed.

coverage_notes: Focused on the regional-council source stream for 2020-2026. CONFIRMED via fetched authoritative pages: Green Lake/Horsham (27 Mar 2024; plus an April 2025 Wimmera-lakes warning), Lake Hamilton/Southern Grampians (28 Feb 2024), Kialla Lakes/Shepparton (7 Nov 2025), Lake Bartlett/Tatura Shepparton (16 Jan 2024), Lake Victoria/Maryborough Central Goldfields (4 Apr 2025 and 13 May 2026), Lake Boga/Swan Hill-GMW (lifted 19 Nov 2025), Lake Neangar/Bendigo (8 Oct 2024), Lake Wallace/West Wimmera (18 Mar 2024 and 19 Mar-12 Jun 2025). Two real but date-unconfirmed events: Lake Bolac/Ararat and Lake Burrumbeet/Ballarat (both councils clearly issued warnings but their pages could not be fetched due to persistent proxy rate-limiting). GAPS / not found in range: Warrnambool (Lake Pertobe) - no council/regional BGA result surfaced; Geelong lakes (Balyang Sanctuary, Eastern Park, Blue Waters, Lake Lorne, St Leonards) - no results; Colac Otway (Lake Colac) - only a 2019 Enterococcus (bacteria, not BGA) page found, no confirmed BGA warning in range; Central Goldfields had 2 additional Lake Victoria pages ('BGA WARNING FOR LAKE VICTORIA' and 'Blue Green Algae present in Lake Victoria') I could not date - likely additional distinct events (2023/2024) not captured here; Shepparton Lake Bartlett has 'levels decline'/status-change follow-ups (end dates) not captured; Ballarat Lake Wendouree, Horsham other lakes, Greater Shepparton Craigmuir Lake, Swan Hill other, Hepburn (Lake Daylesford/Jubilee) - no confirmed council BGA events found. Lake Hume/Bowna Arm exception: WaterNSW/Border Mail red-alert coverage exists (e.g. Bowna Arm red alert 2022) but I did not fetch-confirm dates; left out to avoid unverified records - recommend a dedicated Lake Hume pass. Years covered with confirmed events: 2024 (heaviest), 2025, 2026. 2020-2023 largely a gap in this council stream - the older council pages either 404'd to newer reissues or predated 2020 (e.g. Lake Guthridge Jan 2018, Green Lake Jan 2019 - both out of range and excluded).
caps: WebFetch was severely rate-limited throughout (proxy HTTP 429 'wait ~60s'), and after ~15 successful fetches it effectively stopped returning even after 60-150s sleeps. This blocked confirmation of: Lake Burrumbeet date, Lake Bolac date, the two extra Central Goldfields Lake Victoria pages, Lake Bartlett end date, and the West Wimmera 2025 Lake Wallace issue-date page. WebSearch remained available but its snippets rarely contained dates, and it is US-biased (heavy false positives from Oregon/Kansas/Florida/Canada BC health advisories, all filtered out). Many Victorian regional mastheads (Ararat Advertiser, Gannawarra Times, Wimmera Mallee News) are paywalled. A re-run with a higher WebFetch budget would fill the 2020-2023 gap and the date-unconfirmed events.

label: Statewide media sweep

records.notes: Red alert (avoid contact) issued Mon 24 Feb 2020 for Lake Hume, reported by The Border Mail (published 25 Feb 2020). Article notes a prior 2019 red alert lasted 19 days. Lake Hume straddles VIC/NSW border - included per task exception. No cell count/biovolume stated. Managing authority WaterNSW.

records.authority: Department of Environment, Land, Water and Planning (DELWP/DEECA)
records.notes: Warning issued 14 April 2022 (Nodularia spumigena bloom, nodularin toxin) for parts of the Gippsland Lakes - Lake King and Lake Victoria (Gippsland), incl. Paynesville, Eagle Point, Newlands Arm, Wattle Point, Metung. Advice to avoid contact. Corroborated by VRFish (29 Apr 2022, https://vrfish.com.au/2022/04/29/blue-green-algae-bloom-in-the-gippsland-lakes/) which also lists North Arm and Cunningham Arm. No cell count in cells/mL stated. This Lake Victoria is the Gippsland one, distinct from Lake Victoria in Maryborough.
Red alert (high levels) issued 22 November 2022 for Bowna Arm at Lake Hume by WaterNSW. Included per Lake Hume border exception. No cell count stated on the advisory.

Red alert reported 13 December 2022 by The Border Mail ('another red alert advisory for Lake Hume'). May be a continuation/re-issue of the same late-2022 Lake Hume bloom season as the 22 Nov 2022 Bowna Arm red alert - possible overlap; kept as separate advisory. No cell count stated.
High levels of blue-green algae / avoid-contact warning reported 25 January 2021 (Bendigo Advertiser). GMW-managed reservoir. No cell count stated.

Warning issued 11 January 2023 (high levels). GMW later stated the warning was in place for 160 days (https://www.bendigoadvertiser.com.au/story/8242966/after-160-days-gmw-removes-algae-warning-from-lake-eppalock/), so end_date ~20 June 2023 is APPROXIMATE (calculated, not explicitly stated). No cell count stated.

GMW detected high levels of blue-green algae; warning reported 11 April 2025 (Bendigo Advertiser). Same bloom period still reported active/high on 9-12 May 2025 across central Victorian reservoirs. No cell count stated.

Warning issued around 31 March 2026 (updates through 3 April 2026), over Easter school holidays; described as the 14th warning at Lake Eppalock since records began in 2004. start_date is approximate ('around 31 March 2026'). No cell count stated.
Council warning issued Thursday 14 March 2024 after detecting high levels of blue-green algae at Kialla Lakes; avoid direct contact. No cell count stated.
Council media release: BGA bloom affecting Lake Victoria in Maryborough, issued 4 April 2025. This is the Maryborough Lake Victoria, NOT the Gippsland one. No cell count stated.
Council media release: BGA bloom affecting Lake Victoria in Maryborough, issued 13 May 2026. Distinct event from the April 2025 warning at the same waterbody. No cell count stated.
Council warning issued Friday 19 December 2025 after detecting high levels of blue-green algae at Lake Bartlett in Tatura; avoid direct contact. No cell count stated.

GMW customer letter: BGA warning issued 14 February 2025 for Cairn Curran Reservoir (high, potentially toxic). Still reported at high levels on 9 May 2025 per Bendigo Advertiser (12 May 2025). No cell count/biovolume stated in the letter.
Per Bendigo Advertiser (12 May 2025), Hepburns Lagoon's first blue-green algae warning was issued 9 January 2025. Part of a wider central-Victorian bloom season. No cell count stated.
Per Bendigo Advertiser (12 May 2025), Upper Coliban Reservoir was closed to on-water recreation since 23 April 2025 due to unsafe (high BGA) water. No cell count stated.

Per Bendigo Advertiser (12 May 2025), Laanecoorie Reservoir was among central-Victorian waterbodies with high blue-green algae (levels high as of 9 May 2025). Exact issue date not stated; confirmed active May 2025. No cell count stated.

Maryborough District Advertiser article 'Algae alert in place at Cairn Curran' with URL/title date 2024-02-29 (a 2024 event distinct from the Feb 2025 warning). Date taken from the article URL slug and title; page CONTENT could not be fetched (proxy HTTP 429 throttling on this domain), so not content-verified. GMW notices indicate this bloom also covered Tullaroop Reservoir and Hepburns Lagoon.

City of Ballarat closed Lake Burrumbeet due to toxic blue-green algae (Dolichospermum). Source states a BIOVOLUME of 8.653 mm3/L (NOT cells/mL, so cell_count left null). Closure to remain until testing confirms safety. No publication date exposed on the page and local-radio corroboration (3BA, Power FM) could not be fetched (proxy HTTP 429), so start_date unknown - likely a 2024/2025 event based on page context.

Ararat Rural City Council warning page confirms a blue-green algae warning was issued for Lake Bolac (avoid contact). No date exposed on the council page; Ararat Advertiser and VicEmergency corroboration exist but could not be fetched (proxy HTTP 429). Prior Lake Bolac blooms reported ~2018 and ~2021 per Ararat Advertiser headlines. Date unconfirmed.

Shepparton News reports a blue-green algae warning for Craigmuir Lake in Mooroopna (Greater Shepparton). Article body was paywalled/permission-gated on fetch, so date, severity and cell count could not be confirmed. Date unknown.

Benalla Ensign reports a GMW blue-green algae warning for Lake Nillahcootie (avoid contact, potentially toxic). Related GMW notices show a warning was declared downstream of Lake Nillahcootie on the Broken River and later extended to Shepparton. No publication date exposed in the fetched content, so start_date unknown.

coverage_notes:
Source stream = broad Victorian media/council sweep 2020-2026. Confirmed 22 bloom-warning records by WebFetching the authoritative dated page for each. Filtered OUT all Canadian 'Victoria BC' noise (Times Colonist, vicnews, Saanich, Elk/Thetis/Durrance Lakes, CRD) which appeared frequently.

Year coverage: 2020 (Lake Hume red alert, 1 event); 2021 (Lake Eppalock Jan; Lake Bolac approx); 2022 (Gippsland Lakes Apr; Bowna Arm/Lake Hume Nov-Dec); 2023 (Lake Eppalock Jan-Jun); 2024 (Kialla Lakes Mar; Cairn Curran Feb, low-conf); 2025 (heavy: Hepburns Lagoon Jan, Cairn Curran Feb, Upper Coliban Apr, Lake Eppalock Apr, Lake Victoria Maryborough Apr, Laanecoorie ~May, Lake Bartlett Dec); 2026 (Lake Eppalock ~Mar-Apr; Lake Victoria Maryborough May).

Waterbodies covered: Lake Hume/Bowna Arm (border exception), Gippsland Lakes, Lake Eppalock (4 separate events), Kialla Lakes, Lake Bartlett (Tatura), Craigmuir Lake (Mooroopna), Lake Victoria (Maryborough, 2 events), Cairn Curran Reservoir (2 events), Hepburns Lagoon, Laanecoorie Reservoir, Upper Coliban Reservoir, Lake Nillahcootie, Lake Burrumbeet, Lake Bolac.

High-confidence records rest on council pages / GMW customer-letter PDF / WaterNSW advisories with explicit dates. Medium = dated news reports (Bendigo Advertiser, Border Mail, Dandenong Star). Low = waterbody confirmed as a real warning page but no fetchable date (Lake Burrumbeet, Lake Bolac, Craigmuir Lake, Lake Nillahcootie, Laanecoorie), or date taken only from a URL slug (Cairn Curran 2024).

No cell counts in cells/mL were stated in ANY source - all cell_count fields left null. Lake Burrumbeet gave a biovolume (8.653 mm3/L Dolichospermum), recorded in notes only, not as a cell count.

GAPS: (1) Several regional-news domains (araratadvertiser.com.au, 3ba.com.au, powerfmballarat.com.au, maryboroughadvertiser.com.au) were persistently blocked by the fetch proxy (HTTP 429), leaving Lake Bolac, Lake Burrumbeet and Cairn-Curran-2024 dates unconfirmed. (2) Early years 2020-2021 are thinner in media - likely additional small-lake council warnings exist that weren't surfaced. (3) Warning END/lifted dates are mostly unavailable (councils/news rarely report removal); only Lake Eppalock 2023 has an (approximate, calculated) end date. (4) Many events overlap GMW/MDBA-managed waters (Eppalock, Cairn Curran, Hepburns, Laanecoorie, Nillahcootie, Coliban) and may duplicate those streams; included per 'record any confirmed event' instruction and flagged in notes.
caps: Fetch proxy repeatedly returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) once fetch volume rose, especially for Australian Community Media / regional-radio domains (araratadvertiser, bordermail retries, 3ba, powerfm, maryboroughadvertiser) - several date-confirmation fetches failed entirely and could not be recovered within the session. WebSearch itself never ran dry. Bash curl to the proxy status endpoint was blocked by the environment classifier, so the proxy state could not be inspected.

label: Undated-lake date chase

records.notes: Warning issued 12 Dec 2024 (Greater Shepparton City Council); removed by 6 Jan 2025 (removal announcement date) — exact removal date not stated.
Warning issued ~1 May 2024 per Ararat Rural City Council; Parks Victoria cautioned avoid-contact. Removal date not found.
Lake closed after Dolichospermum at biovolume 8.653 mm3/L (biovolume, not cells/mL, so cell count null); council notice published 21 Mar 2025. Removal date not found.
Current warning still active mid-2026; issue date 13 Nov 2025 per GMW live alert feed. Distinct from the 2025 bloom removed 4 Sep 2025.

coverage_notes: Follow-up dating of previously undated blooms
caps: Edwardes Lake and the WaterNSW Hume Frist Bay 2023-24 event could not be dated precisely.
